title: neomammillaria collina
intro: Neomammillaria collina, also known as the 'hill cactus', is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been used to treat wounds, burns, and skin infections. The health benefits of Neomammillaria collina include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and promote skin regeneration, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Neomammillaria collina can be made into a topical ointment, salve, or infusion, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health applications.
